sections.heading: Preamble
sections.text:
Teaching Profession (Amendment) [No. 4 of 2023 79
GOVERNMENT OF ZAMBIA
ACT
No. 4 of 2023
Date of Assent: 14th April, 2023
An Act to amend the Teaching Profession Act.
[ 18th April, 2023
ENACTED by the Parliament of Zambia. Enactment
1

sections.heading: This Act may be cited as the Teaching Profession Short title
sections.text:
(Amendment) Act, 2023, and shall be read as one with the Teaching Act No. 5 of
Profession Act, 2013, in this Act referred to as the principal Act. 2013
2

sections.heading: Section 2 of the principal Act is amended by the insertion Amendment
sections.text:
of the following new definitions in the appropriate places in of section 2
alphabetical order:
“Higher Education Authority” means the Higher Education
Authority established under the Higher Education Act, Act No. 4 of
2013; and 2013
“Zambia Qualifications Authority” means the Zambia
Qualifications Authority established under the Zambia Act No. 13
Qualifications Authority Act, 2011;. of 2011
3

sections.heading: Section 6 (1) of the principal Act is amended by the— Amendment
sections.text:
of section 6
(a) deletion of paragraph (c);
(b) deletion of paragraph (d) and the substitution therefor
of the following:
(d) promote continuing professional development
among teachers;; and
(c) renumbering of paragraphs (d), (e), (f), (g), (h), (i), (j),
(k), (l), (m) and (n) as paragraphs (c), (d), (e), (f), (g),
(h), (i), (j), (k), (l) and (m), respectively.
Single copies of this Act may be obtained from the Government Printer,
P.O. Box 30136, 10101 Lusaka, Price K4.00 each.

80 No. 4 of 2023] Teaching Profession (Amendment)

of section 11

(b) holds a qualification accredited and recognised by the

subsections (1), (2) and (3), respectively.

replacement and the substitution therefor of the following:
of section 36
Continuing 36. The Minister may, by statutory instrument, on the
professional recommendation of the Council, make Regulations
development to provide for—
(a) continuing professional development to be
undertaken by teachers; and
(b) the criteria for the recognition by the Council of
continuing professional development.
Repeal of 8. Section 37 of the principal Act is repealed.
section 37

Part V
Amendment 10. Section 59 (2) of the principal Act is amended—
of section 59

“accredited and recognised by the Zambia

word “qualifications”; and
